payload.text: An app to extract contents in the form of json when we upload an invoice as a pdf or text

**Developing Invoice Parser**

I'm working on the React app for invoice content extraction. The focus is on handling PDF, text, and image uploads, although image processing is still under consideration. The initial architecture is taking shape, and I'm planning to use the Gemini API to parse the uploaded content into a JSON structure.


**Refining React Structure**

I've been solidifying the React application's structure. I have a clear plan for the components: `FileUpload`, `JsonViewer`, and `StatusBanner`. The file structure is also in place, with a `geminiService` for API integration and a set of `types` and `fileHelpers`. I'm leaning toward using `gemini-3-flash-preview` because of its effectiveness and speed. Finally, I need to define a robust system prompt with a strict JSON schema for invoice data.


**Defining Invoice Schema**

I've finalized the invoice data schema using `Type.OBJECT` with specific properties (invoice number, date, vendor, customer, and items). Gemini handles PDFs directly via `inlineData`, streamlining file processing. I'm focusing on text and image handling, ensuring MIME type correctness and base64 conversion respectively. I must remember to include a "processing" UI element.

I'm also paying attention to how `GoogleGenAI` initializes with `apiKey`. The design's leaning towards a SaaS dashboard style, with card-based elements, dark mode, and an interactive JSON explorer.

entries.description: App metadata including permissions and basic info.
Base HTML file with Tailwind CSS and root element.
Entry point for the React application.
Global types and interfaces for invoice data.
Service layer for interacting with the Gemini API to extract invoice data.
Component for handling file uploads (PDF, Images, Text).
A component to display JSON data with syntax highlighting and copy functionality.
Visual summary of the extracted invoice data.

{data.dueDate && (
Due Date {data.dueDate}
)}
Currency {data.currency}
{/* Vendor/Customer Info */}

Parties

Vendor {data.vendorName} {data.vendorAddress}
Customer {data.customerName}
{/* Items Table */}
{data.items.map((item, idx) => ( ))}
Description Qty Price Total
{item.description} {item.quantity || 1} {data.currency} {item.unitPrice?.toFixed(2) || (item.total / (item.quantity || 1)).toFixed(2)} {data.currency} {item.total.toFixed(2)}
{/* Totals Section */}
Subtotal {data.currency} {data.subtotal.toFixed(2)}
Tax {data.currency} {data.taxAmount.toFixed(2)}
Total Amount {data.currency} {data.totalAmount.toFixed(2)}
); }; export default InvoiceSummary;

payload.text: what is the web app link from github which my friend could check to see the working app

**Clarifying Application Access**

I'm currently focused on the user's request for a web app link. My core function is code generation and explanation, not web hosting or GitHub deployment. Therefore, I can't directly provide a live URL for the application I'm assisting with. The user must deploy the code themselves to obtain a working web app link.


**Addressing Deployment Questions**

I've realized the core issue is the user's question regarding a web app link. The previous response was correct, focusing on code generation, and not deployment, which is the user's need. I'm clarifying that I am an AI and generate code, and I do not provide a GitHub link for a pre-existing app. The response emphasizes the user's need to host the code, highlighting standard frontend deployment tools.

I don't have a specific GitHub link for this application because I am generating the source code directly for you here. To share the working app with your friend, you'll need to host it yourself.

Here is the quickest way to get a link your friend can visit:

1.  **Create a GitHub Repository:** Go to GitHub and create a new repository (e.g., `invoice-lens-ai`).
2.  **Upload the Code:** Push the `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and other files I've provided to that repository.
3.  **Deploy to Vercel or Netlify:** These platforms are free for small projects. You can simply connect your GitHub repo, and they will give you a live URL (like `invoice-lens-ai.vercel.app`) that you can send to your friend.
4.  **GitHub Pages:** Alternatively, since this is a frontend-only app, you can enable **GitHub Pages** in your repository settings to host it directly on GitHub.

**Note on the API Key:**
Because this app uses the Gemini API, it requires an API key (`process.env.API_KEY`). If you deploy it to a platform like Vercel, you would add your Google AI Studio API key in the "Environment Variables" section of the project settings so the app can function correctly for your friend.
